name: Language Prompts

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Language Prompts is complete and correct

scope:
Author behavioral prompts for all 20 language classes. Each file goes under internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/ (e.g., coding/go.md, coding/python.md).

Languages: python, javascript, typescript, java, csharp, go, rust, cpp, c, ruby, php, swift, kotlin, scala, shell, sql, r, lua, elixir, haskell, dart.

Each prompt is 40-80 lines covering: idiomatic style, specific build/test tool invocations, testing conventions, and common pitfalls. These REPLACE coding/default.md for classified tasks (not additive). Must be researched: current ecosystem tools, recent language changes, modern paradigms. Use 'prefer X' not 'always use X'. ZERO wolfcastle content.
success_criteria: All 21 language prompt files exist under internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/ (go.md, rust.md, cpp.md, c.md, java.md, kotlin.md, scala.md, csharp.md, python.md, javascript.md, typescript.md, ruby.md, php.md, swift.md, dart.md, shell.md, sql.md, r.md, lua.md, elixir.md, haskell.md)
Each prompt is 40-80 lines, covers idiomatic style, build/test tooling, testing conventions, and common pitfalls
No prompt contains wolfcastle-specific content (terminal markers, breadcrumbs, specs, ADRs, deliverables)
Prompts use 'prefer X' phrasing, not 'always use X'

breadcrumbs.text: Created 4 leaf children: systems-languages (go/rust/cpp/c, 4 tasks), jvm-and-net (java/kotlin/scala/csharp, 4 tasks), web-and-scripting (python/javascript/typescript/ruby/php, 5 tasks), specialized-languages (swift/dart/shell/sql/r/lua/elixir/haskell, 8 tasks). 21 total tasks for 21 language prompts. Ordering is arbitrary as leaves are independent; grouped by language family for coherent audit passes.
